topic: A student is accused of plagiarism
raw_output:
[story 1]

The accusation landed like a physical blow. I stared at the printed page, the damning highlighted sections mocking me from the desk. My hands clenched into tight fists, the knuckles turning white under the pressure. Every word they cited felt like a deliberate assault, a calculated dismantling of the effort I’d poured into this work. "This is theft," the professor had stated, his voice flat and devoid of any nuance. I could feel the heat rising in my chest, a tight, burning sensation that made it hard to draw a full breath. I wanted to shout, to lash out, but the silence of the sterile office room held me captive. My jaw locked so hard I could feel the strain in my neck.

[story 2]

I walked into the department meeting feeling a cold, hard knot tightening in my stomach. When the accusations were laid out on the screen, the room seemed to shrink. I gripped the strap of my backpack until my fingers ached, focusing on the pattern of the cheap carpet beneath my shoes. My gaze kept flicking toward the accusation sheet, my eyes narrowed to slits. When the committee began questioning, my voice came out tight and low, clipped with an unusual sharpness. "There is a misunderstanding," I managed, the words tasting like ash. I could feel the blood pounding behind my eyes, a frantic, insistent rhythm that demanded release. I wanted to slam my palm onto the table, to make a scene, anything to disrupt this suffocating atmosphere.

[story 3]

The email arrived at 2 AM. It was a sterile notification detailing the alleged academic dishonesty. I read it three times, the fluorescent light of my room buzzing overhead seeming to amplify the tension in the air. A tremor ran through my hands as I set the screen down. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my bed and the window. My breath hitched, coming in short, shallow gasps. The injustice of it was a corrosive acid eating away at my composure. I kicked at a discarded textbook, the sound sharp and jarring. I needed to dismantle this narrative, to show them the sheer effort involved, but all I could manage was a rigid stillness, a coiled tension vibrating just beneath my skin.
